notes: This batch treats oprole8 as a standalone fixed-role harness family with a direct paired result surface.
run_sim_suite_v1.py, run_sim_suite_v2_full_axes.py, and results_S_SIM_AXIS56_OPERATOR4_LR_SUITE_V1.json were read only as comparison anchors.
possible_downstream_consequence_policy: possible downstream consequence only; no direct A2-1 or A1 promotion from this batch

comparison_anchors.relevance: Shows oprole8 and the later operator4 descendant as separate micro operator-role surfaces.
Contains the later operator4 descendant evidence block and exposes the current code-hash mismatch against the current emitter file.
Current hash matches the evidence-pack code hash for the later operator4 descendant, even though current main does not emit that SIM_ID.
Current emitter of S_SIM_AXIS56_OPERATOR4_LR_SUITE_V1 and its paired result.
Later operator-role result surface used as comparison-only descendant anchor.

runtime_expectations: run_oprole8_left_right_suite.py runs 256 random-density trials, measures left-vs-right delta traces and commutator norms for four fixed role matrices, writes results_oprole8_left_right_suite.json, and emits one local SIM_EVIDENCE block.
The paired oprole8 result surface stores one nested metrics object and omits explicit seed or trial metadata.
The later operator4 comparison suite uses TI/TE/FI/FE superoperators, 512 trials, and a flat result schema.
failure_modes: The placeholder role labels R1/R2/R3/R4 can be mistaken for the final TI/TE/FI/FE operator taxonomy.
Catalog co-location with the operator4 descendant can be mistaken for one clean rename lineage.

The current evidence-pack code hash for S_SIM_AXIS56_OPERATOR4_LR_SUITE_V1 can be mistaken for a clean current producer path even though it matches run_sim_suite_v1.py rather than the current emitter run_sim_suite_v2_full_axes.py.

sims_split_rule.notes: This batch remains inside the separate sims source class and does not mix with ordinary theory/doc extraction.
